title: Umananda Temple
tagline: Umananda Temple — spiritual town of Assam

overview.short: Umananda Devaloi (Pron: ˈʊməˌnændə ˈdeɪvəˌlɔɪ) is a Shiva temple located on the Umananda Island (Peacock Island) in the middle of the river Brahmaputra, Assam.
overview.description: Umananda Devaloi (Pron: ˈʊməˌnændə ˈdeɪvəˌlɔɪ) is a Shiva temple located on the Umananda Island (Peacock Island) in the middle of the river Brahmaputra, Assam. Brahmaputra just opposite the office of the Deputy Commissioner of Kamrup or the Kachari Ghat in Guwahati. It is known as the smallest inhabited riverine island in the world. Country boats that are available on the bank of Brahmaputra take the visitors to the island.

nearestAirport.name: Lokpriya Gopinath Bordoloi International Airport, Guwahati

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Umananda Island
topPlaces.description: Umananda Island is the smallest inhabited river islet in the middle of river Brahmaputra, flowing through the city of Guwahati in Assam, a state in northeast India. Its name derives from Assamese Uma, another name for the Hindu goddess Parvati, the wife of Shiva; and ananda, which translates to "joy".

topPlaces.name: Judges Field
topPlaces.description: Judges Field or Gauhati Town Club Ground is a multi-purpose sports ground in Guwahati, Assam. The ground is mainly used for football and cricket matches.

topPlaces.name: Nehru Park, Guwahati
topPlaces.description: Nehru Park (Assamese pronunciation: [nɛɦɹu uˈdjan]) is an urban park situated in the Pan Bazaar area of Guwahati, India.

topPlaces.name: Dighalipukhuri
topPlaces.description: Dighalipukhuri; also Dighalipukhri is a rectangular man-made pond in Guwahati, Assam, India, about half a mile long. Dighalipukhuri is occupied altogether area of garden and pond between 17-18 bigha lands.

topPlaces.name: Guwahati Ropeway
topPlaces.description: Guwahati Ropeway is an aerial tramway system crossing the Brahmaputra River in Guwahati, Assam, India. Inaugurated on 24 August 2020, it connects the city centre at Kachari Ghat in Panbazar with Rajadwar near the Doul Govinda Temple in North Guwahati.

topPlaces.name: Northbrook Gate
topPlaces.description: The Northbrook Gate, was constructed to welcome British viceroy Lord Northbrook who visited Guwahati in 1874. The gate was built near Sukreswar Ghat where the viceroy anchored his ship.

topPlaces.name: Ugratara Devalaya
topPlaces.description: Ugratara Devalaya is a temple dedicated to Ugratara located in the western side of Jor Pukhury tanks in the heart of Guwahati city in the Lotaxil (Latasil) locality in Northeast India. The Ugratara Temple in Uzan Bazaar in the eastern part of Guwahati, Assam, is a Shakti shrine.

topPlaces.name: Doul Govinda Temple
topPlaces.description: Doul Govinda Temple (Pron: ˈdaʊl/ˈdu:l/ˈdəʊl gə(ʊ)ˈvɪndə) is one of the important temples of Kamrup, Assam, India. It is situated on the northern banks, on the foothills of Chandra Bharati hill at Rajaduar, North Guwahati.

items.note: Umananda Island is the smallest inhabited river islet in the middle of river Brahmaputra, flowing through the city of Guwahati in Assam, a state in northeast India. Its name derives from Assamese Uma, another name for the Hindu goddess Parvati, the wife of Shiva; and ananda, which translates to "joy".
Judges Field or Gauhati Town Club Ground is a multi-purpose sports ground in Guwahati, Assam. The ground is mainly used for football and cricket matches.
Nehru Park (Assamese pronunciation: [nɛɦɹu uˈdjan]) is an urban park situated in the Pan Bazaar area of Guwahati, India.

Dighalipukhuri; also Dighalipukhri is a rectangular man-made pond in Guwahati, Assam, India, about half a mile long. Dighalipukhuri is occupied altogether area of garden and pond between 17-18 bigha lands.

Guwahati Ropeway is an aerial tramway system crossing the Brahmaputra River in Guwahati, Assam, India. Inaugurated on 24 August 2020, it connects the city centre at Kachari Ghat in Panbazar with Rajadwar near the Doul Govinda Temple in North Guwahati.
The Northbrook Gate, was constructed to welcome British viceroy Lord Northbrook who visited Guwahati in 1874. The gate was built near Sukreswar Ghat where the viceroy anchored his ship.

Ugratara Devalaya is a temple dedicated to Ugratara located in the western side of Jor Pukhury tanks in the heart of Guwahati city in the Lotaxil (Latasil) locality in Northeast India. The Ugratara Temple in Uzan Bazaar in the eastern part of Guwahati, Assam, is a Shakti shrine.

Doul Govinda Temple (Pron: ˈdaʊl/ˈdu:l/ˈdəʊl gə(ʊ)ˈvɪndə) is one of the important temples of Kamrup, Assam, India. It is situated on the northern banks, on the foothills of Chandra Bharati hill at Rajaduar, North Guwahati.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Umananda Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Umananda Temple is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Umananda Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Lokpriya Gopinath Bordoloi International Airport, Guwahati (19 km) and the nearest railway station is Guwahati Railway Station (2 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Umananda Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Umananda Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Umananda Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Umananda Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Umananda Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Umananda Island, Judges Field, Nehru Park, Guwahati, Dighalipukhuri, Guwahati Ropeway.
